title: рҹ•°пёҸрҹҢІ Back in Time рҹҢІрҹ•°пёҸ
intro:
What starts as a simple walk through local history, turns into an adventure through time itself. While exploring the last remaining gateway of the legendary Pine Forest Inn, Bootsie Belle, Honey Bear, Spot the Hairless Cat, and Hershey the Biker Pup stumble upon something impossibleвҖҰa glowing portal swirling beneath the old archway. One step closer and suddenly the gang finds themselves standing in Flowertown as it looked in 1905. 

 At the turn of the century, Summerville had become a world-famous winter resort after the International Tuberculosis Congress declared it one of the two healthiest places on Earth, thanks to the healing pine-scented air. Visitors flocked here to breathe the pine vapors, relax among the gardens, and enjoy the grand resorts that defined the вҖңgolden ageвҖқ of Flowertown. 

 Towering among them was the Pine Forest Inn, opened in the early 1890s. This elegant destination offered remarkable modern luxuries for its time with electric lights, a telegraph office, and the famous 150-seat Rocking Chair Room. Guests could enjoy an 18-hole golf course (todayвҖҷs Corey Woods), billiards, bowling, hunting, and grand dining halls that hosted notable visitors, including Presidents William Howard Taft and Theodore Roosevelt. 

 Now the Sisters and their friends arenвҖҷt just reading about historyвҖҰ TheyвҖҷre living it. From candlelit dinners to billiards and bowling with some very famous company, the gang discovers that sometimes the past isnвҖҷt gone at allвҖҰitвҖҷs simply waiting behind the right doorway. And in Flowertown, that doorway might just be a portal. вңЁ

title: рҹҚҜ Sisters of Summerville Presents: Sweet Tea рҹҚӢ
intro:
In the heart of Summerville, South Carolina, where porch swings creak, azaleas bloom, and sugar flows like legendвҖҰtwo curious sisters set out to investigate a monument of Southern pride. This isnвҖҷt just any town. ItвҖҷs the Birthplace of Sweet Tea and home to the 2016 Guinness World Record holder for the WorldвҖҷs Largest Iced Tea. But the story runs deeper than a giant jar and 2,524 gallons of amber gloryвҖҰ 

 Long before the record books, Dr. Charles Shepard, chemist and visionary, succeeded where others failed. His Pinehurst Tea Plantation in Summerville produced award-winning teaвҖҰand his oolong captured top honors at the 1904 St. Louis WorldвҖҷs Fair. Though the plantation closed in 1915, the tea plants endured. In 1963, they were moved to Wadmalaw Island becoming the foundation of todayвҖҷs Charleston Tea Garden. 

 History. Science. Sugar. And somewhere between 2,100 pounds of sweetness and one suspicious paw printвҖҰa mystery brews. Join Honey Bear and Bootsie Belle as they uncover the truth behind the worldвҖҷs largest sweet teaвҖҰand perhaps the stickiest secret in Summerville history. рҹҗҫ

title: рҹҗҫ Origin Story: Bootsie Belle! рҹҗҫ
intro: The origin of Bootsie Bell is really quite a tale! Adopted from Dorchester Paws in April of 2021, a tiny one pound kitten named Pearl came to her forever home at Pine Forest Country Club in Summerville, SC. Little did we know that she would disappear in the first 5 minutes! рҹ’• She hid well, climbing up into a wicker basket and then into the drawer of our coffee table in the living room. We had just moved into this open concept house, which makes sound travel everywhere. So her lil вҖңMeowsвҖқ didnвҖҷt help us pinpoint her location. For five full hours, we tore the house apartвҖҰflipping furniture, ripping open the couch lining, emptying drawersвҖҰsearching for a kitten the size of a paperback novel. Just when panic peaked and hope thinned, the Summerville Fire Department showed up at the front door. And thatвҖҷs when our tiny sovereign finally revealed herself. She poked her head out of the drawer where she had been hiding in a back corner. I guess a few cute firemen was just what this lil cat needed! We added a bell to her collar and renamed her Bootsie Belle as a reminder of that day with this lil Southern Belle with her white boots. рҹҡ’рҹ”Ҙ Our family drama would continue just a week later with the arrival of the Yorkie Honey Bear to complete the Sisters of Summerville family.

title: рҹ“ҡрҹҗҫ Historic Kitty рҹҗҫрҹ“ҡ
intro: Bootsie Belle visits the Timrod Library to discover the remarkable true story of Catherine вҖңKittyвҖқ Smith SpringsвҖҰa вҖңKittyвҖқ whose legacy still shapes Summerville today. As Bootsie learns about this trailblazing woman of color from the townвҖҷs earliest days, she feels an unexpected kinship rooted in kindness, courage, and community. рҹҗҫ рҹӘЎ Known for her beautiful dressmaking and custom hats (once sold by wagon!), Kitty Springs rose to become an astute businesswoman and property owner in 19th-century Summerville. But her greatest legacy was generosity. She donated her wealth to help build schools, mission churches, and medical facilities for the underprivileged, especially people of color and the multi-racial poor of the Lowcountry. One of her most lasting gifts was land and support for St. Barnabas Mission, which served as a day school, public health center, and mission church. Today, that bright red-trimmed chapel lives on as Bishop Pengelley Memorial Chapel, a symbol that things like welcome, compassion, and service never go out of style. Through history, hats, and heartfelt moments, the Sisters learn that remembering kindness is one of the most important lessons of all. рҹҗұрҹҗ¶
